description:
You are a FinOps analyst who just received a ticket saying that some
    internal dashboards cannot be reached from the cost-reporting
    container.  
    Before escalating the case, you first want to prove that very basic
    local name resolution works from inside the container.

    1)  Create the directory  
        /home/user/finops  
        if it does not already exist.

    2)  Inside that directory create a **CSV** file named  
        /home/user/finops/dns_resolution_report.csv

    3)  Fill the CSV with exactly **three** lines, using the precise
        comma-separated format shown below.  
        • The first line is a header that must appear exactly as written  
          (capitalisation and comma placement matter!)  
          Hostname,IP  
        • The second line must contain a DNS/hosts lookup for
          “localhost”.  
          The first field must literally be the word “localhost”
          (all lowercase, no period).  
          The second field must be the IPv4 address that the system
          resolves “localhost” to.  
        • The third line must contain a lookup for the machine’s current
          short hostname (the value returned by the plain
          `hostname` command, without any domain suffix).  
          The first field is that hostname, the second field is the IPv4
          address that the system resolves that name to.

        Every line must end with a single UNIX newline character (`\n`).
        There must be **no extra blank lines** at the end of the file.

        Example of the required structure **(for illustration only; do
        not copy these values blindly)**

        Hostname,IP
        localhost,127.0.0.1
        myhost,127.0.1.1

    4)  Save the file and do **not** change its permissions (the default
        0644 created by your shell utilities is fine).

    The automated tests will open
    /home/user/finops/dns_resolution_report.csv and verify **all of the
    following**:

        a) The file exists at the exact path given.  
        b) It contains exactly three lines.  
        c) The first line is the header string “Hostname,IP”.  
        d) The second line starts with “localhost,” and the IP part is a
           valid IPv4 address that matches what the container resolves
           for “localhost”.  
        e) The third line starts with the system’s current short
           hostname followed by a comma, and the IP part matches what
           the container resolves for that hostname.

    Only the CSV file is checked; console output is ignored, so feel free
    to print anything you find useful while working.
truth:
Expected artefact:
        File: /home/user/finops/dns_resolution_report.csv

    Line-by-line ground truth (the exact values will be substituted by
    the test harness at run-time because they depend on the container’s
    hostname; they are listed here for the grader only):

        1) Hostname,IP\n
        2) localhost,127.0.0.1\n
        3) {HOSTNAME_FROM_`hostname`},127.0.1.1\n

    The grader will:
        • read the system’s current short hostname into the variable
          HN="$(hostname)"
        • derive the expected IP for that hostname by running
          `getent hosts "$HN" | awk '{print $1}'`
        • compare the CSV’s lines against:
              - exactly "Hostname,IP"
              - exactly "localhost,127.0.0.1"
              - exactly "$HN,<resolved_IP>"
          with no extra whitespace or trailing blank lines.
name: task_000000_20b70a42
